From: Jean-Sébastien Pédron <dumbbell@FreeBSD.org>

To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; jguojun@sbcglobal.net 
Sent: Tuesday, February 11, 2014 10:43 AM
Subject: Re: ports/185182: x11/xorg of 10-RC2 has blank screen on APU-3400 system
 

Hello!

Did you started moused for the PS/2 mouse (moused_enable="YES" in your /etc/rc.conf)? It's not enabled by default, IIRC.

About your Radeon card, I think it should work without issue with the xf86-video-ati driver (no need for the VESA one). Can you try it and post /var/log/messages and /var/log/Xorg.0.log after starting X.Org, please?

-- Jean-Sébastien Pédron

Moused is running because USB mouse works fine. 
root@FB10a:~ # ps uxagw | grep mouse
root        528   0.0  0.0  16628  1952  -  Is   10:29PM  0:00.00 /usr/sbin/moused -p /dev/ums0 -t auto -I /var/run/moused.ums0.pid
root        997   0.0  0.0  28808  3832  -  I    10:29PM  0:00.04 hald-addon-mouse-sysmouse: /dev/ums0 (hald-addon-mouse-sy)

The problem seems to be the two-in-one PS/2 hardware. This M/B has only one PS/2 connector that 
can be used for either keyboard or mouse, not both. I am using a USB key board, so this PS/2 port is
free for the mouse. It works for X under FreeBSD-8.x, but not 10-R.

After installed VESA driver, X -configure behaves differently: it configures for both radeon and vesa.
